2606.07006.title: RASFT: Rollout-Adaptive Supervised Fine-Tuning for Reasoning
2606.07006.paper_summary: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) is a prevailing method for adapting large language models to reasoning tasks by imitating offline expert demonstrations, often treating a single expert trajectory as the target behavior. However, reasoning is not simple path imitation: rigidly following one demonstrated solution may overfit to surface forms and suppress the model's own reasoning distribution. We propose Rollout-Adaptive Supervised Fine-Tuning (RASFT), a policy-aware SFT framework that calibrates expert supervision according to problem-level solvability estimated from verified on-policy rollouts. For each problem, RASFT strengthens expert guidance when the current policy struggles, while relaxing rigid imitation and incorporating correct self-generated trajectories when the model already exhibits reliable reasoning behavior. To preserve useful reasoning priors, RASFT further introduces a clipped inverse ratio between the frozen reference model and the current policy to constrain excessive policy drift. Experiments across multiple models on six mathematical reasoning benchmarks and two code reasoning benchmarks show that RASFT achieves better overall performance than SFT, SFT variants, and representative RL methods. The code is available at https://github.com/zjd1sq/RASFT.
2606.07006.paper_summary_zh: 監督微調（SFT）是一種流行的方法，用於通過模仿離線專家示範來將大型語言模型適應於推理任務，通常將單一專家軌跡視為目標行為。<lb/>然而，推理並不是簡單的路徑模仿：僵硬地遵循一個示範解決方案可能會過度擬合於表面形式，並抑制模型自身的推理分佈。<lb/>我們提出了滾動自適應監督微調（RASFT），這是一個政策感知的SFT框架，根據從已驗證的政策回放中估計的問題級可解性來校準專家監督。<lb/>對於每個問題，當當前政策遇到困難時，RASFT增強專家指導，同時在模型已經展現出可靠的推理行為時，放鬆僵硬的模仿並納入正確的自生成軌跡。<lb/>為了保留有用的推理先驗，RASFT進一步引入了凍結參考模型與當前政策之間的剪切逆比，以限制過度的政策漂移。<lb/>在六個數學推理基準和兩個代碼推理基準上對多個模型進行的實驗表明，RASFT的整體性能優於SFT、SFT變體和代表性的強化學習方法。<lb/>代碼可在 https://github.com/zjd1sq/RASFT 獲得。
2606.07006.author: Yongliang Miao et.al.
2606.07006.authors: Yongliang Miao, Fengyuan Liu, Wei Shi, Yanguang Liu, Fei Sun, Na Zou, Mengnan Du
